meaning: v.唱轻快的调子,轻快地动n.轻快活泼的调子,轻快的动作
examples: Her voice had a soft Welsh lilt to it.
Her voice is child-like, with a West Country lilt.
The voices he heard in the Bronx never had Naomi’s lilt.
Her questions ended not in an inquisitorial lilt but with a descending, matter-of-fact thud.
Julie Freeman has luminous eyes and the captivating lilt of a natural storyteller.
